__readme__: Kairos places import (Places → Import). Required: "places" array; each item has "slug" (for logs) and "place" object. Optional top-level "source". Delete __readme__ if you want; importer only reads "places" and "source". Writes to browser localStorage (kairos-places-v1); no timeline events are created. place.name: required string. place.id: optional. If set to an existing place UUID, this row replaces that record (upsert). If omitted, a new id is generated. place.region: optional subtitle (e.g. Canaan). place.description: optional long text. place.scriptureAppearances: [{ "book": "Joshua", "chapter": 6 }] — same book names as People import; invalid rows are ignored in the UI. place.relatedTimelineEventIds: optional array of existing timeline event UUIDs from your library (links only; events are not created here). place.atlasPin: optional { catalogMapId, nx, ny } — catalogMapId is a workspace atlas id from bible-map (e.g. ws-05-the-land-of-canaan); nx/ny are 0–1 normalized position on that plate image. place.imageDataUrl: optional full data URL for a photo.
source: Example places pack — replace with your dataset name

place.name: Jericho
place.description: Ancient walled city; conquest narrative in Joshua; Zacchaeus and the road in the Gospels.

place.name: Shiloh
place.description: Location of the tabernacle in Judges and 1 Samuel.
